rulings.Case Title: ELEN MEDER VS. STATE OF CALIFORNIA - HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, August 16, 2019, Line 1, PETITIONER ELEN MEDER'S Petition and Notice Of Hearing. Off calendar. Petitioner served the Governor with the petition and notice of hearing. Those documents should have been served on the Department of Social Services. (See Code of Civil Procedure Secs. 1107; 416.50.) The court suggests that petitioner contact the Department of Social Services to determine whether it will allow the Attorney General's office to accept service. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: BORREGAS COURT ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA GENERAL VS. BOSTON FINANCIAL INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT A ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Edison Capital Housing Partners, Xi, Llc For Protective Order Regarding Plaintiff Borregas Court Associates Second And Third Sets Of Special Interrogatories; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 16, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANT BFIM-CALIFORNIA ACQUISITION CORP., ERRONEOUSLY, EDISON CAPITAL HOUSING INVESTMENTS, EDISON CAPITAL HOUSING PARTNERS, XI, LLC's Motion Of Defendant Edison Capital Housing Partners, Xi, Llc For Protective Order Regarding Plaintiff Borregas Court Associates Second And Third Sets Of Special Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Julia Campins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. Plaintiff has failed to explain how this discovery is necessary to the prosecution of the case and could not be sought through less burdensome means. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to julia@campinsbenhambaker.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER WUERFEL EMORY ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 16, 2019, Line 4, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted. Pro Tem Judge Julia Campins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion Granted. The Court requests that Defendant City and County of San Francisco make greater efforts to locate the Plaintiffs to determine whether this case should proceed at all, given the apparent lack of participation since removal of counsel. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to julia@campinsbenhambaker.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KAREN BOGDANYI VS. HEALTH ADVOCATES LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, August 16, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT HEALTH ADVOCATES LLC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Health Advocates, LLC's demurrer is overruled in its entirety. Plaintiff no longer simply parrots the language of the Labor Code but pleads specific factual examples regarding how defendant systematically violated the Labor Code. (Second Amended Complaint Paras. 16, 33, 38, 39, 61.) Plaintiff pleads facts showing an ascertainable and sufficiently numerous class and a well-defined community of interest. (See Tarkington v. California Unemployment Ins. Appeals Bd. (2009) 172 Cal.App.4th 1494, 1511 [recognizing that resolution of class issues on the pleadings is apt to occur in a mass tort action rather than a wage and hour action].) The court further notes that a class action is not inappropriate merely because each member of the class may at some point be required to make an individual showing as to his or her eligibility to recover. (See Bell v. Farmers Ins. Exchange (2004) 115 Cal.App.4th 715, 742.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES SALES VS. BANK OF AMERICA
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, August 16, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the demurrer was unopposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
